title: crataegus pedicellata var. caesa
intro: Crataegus pedicellata var. caesa, also known as the hawthorn tree, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Rosaceae family, native to the Mediterranean region. The medicinal properties of Crataegus pedicellata var. caesa lie in its flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins, and other bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to promote cardiovascular health. The health benefits of this plant include lowering blood pressure, improving heart function, and reducing the risk of heart disease, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Crataegus pedicellata var. caesa can be prepared as herbal teas, capsules, or extracts, and is also used in traditional medicine to support overall cardiovascular well-being.
